title: 怪屋
summary:
After a conversation with a woman who expresses her gratitude, Charles is informed by Sophia that his father wishes for him to return to London to visit Scotland Yard. Charles meets his father in an oppressive office alongside Superintendent Tavener, Inspector Taverna, and Mr. Gaskill.

The group discusses a significant development involving Mr. Agrotopoulos, the owner of the Telfi restaurant and a longtime associate of the late Aristides Leonidis. As instructed by Aristides over a year ago, Agrotopoulos has delivered a sealed letter and a new will. This document differs significantly from the previous version. Mr. Gaskill expresses resentment toward the deceased, feeling that Aristides' actions were intended to make him appear incompetent.

Charles learns that the new will leaves the majority of Aristides' movable and immovable property to his granddaughter, Sophia Katherine Leontides, rather than dividing it among his other relatives. Charles, who intends to marry Sophia, reads an explanatory letter written by Aristides. In the letter, Aristides explains that he believes every family requires a dominant person to bear the burden of its welfare. He asserts that his sons, Roger and Philip, and his grandson, Eustace, lack the necessary business acumen, confidence, or judgment to lead. He identifies Sophia as the only heir possessing the brains, courage, and impartiality required to protect the family and his sister-in-law, Edith de Harville. Aristides admits the will is a "trap" designed to pass the family burden to Sophia without humiliating his other heirs.
